form.subtitle: Pelvic venous disorders — reflux, obstruction & congestion

renal.title: Left renal vein (nutcracker screening)
renal.subtitle: Ultrasound is screening only — confirm with CT/MR venography.

caval.title: Iliac & caval veins (May-Thurner / DVT)
caval.subtitle: Obstruction is screening only — confirm with IVUS / CT venography.

diagram.title: Pelvic venous map
diagram.subtitle: Draw findings and add text labels directly on the illustration.

narrative.ovarianReflux: {side}: dilated, refluxing ovarian vein ({diameterMm} mm, reflux {durationS} s).
narrative.plexusCongestion: {side}: congested pelvic venous plexus (largest vein {diameterMm} mm).
narrative.normalSide: {side}: no significant pelvic venous reflux or obstruction.

conclusion.nutcracker: Left renal vein compression — nutcracker phenomenon suggested (screening).
conclusion.confirmRenal: Confirm renal vein compression with CT/MR venography.
conclusion.mayThurner: {side} common iliac vein compression — May-Thurner pattern (screening).
conclusion.confirmIliac: Confirm iliac obstruction with IVUS or CT/MR venography.
conclusion.pelvicCongestion: Pelvic venous reflux consistent with pelvic congestion.
conclusion.normal: No pelvic venous reflux or obstruction identified.

actions.applyTemplateConfirmBody: This will replace current findings, impression, and recommendations.
actions.enterFindingFirst: Enter at least one finding before exporting.
actions.newCaseConfirmBody: This will erase all current data. Unsaved changes will be lost.

normal.name: Normal pelvic venous study
normal.description: No reflux or obstruction in any zone.
normal.impression: No pelvic venous reflux or obstruction identified. Gonadal veins of normal calibre without reflux; no iliac vein compression or thrombosis; left renal vein without nutcracker features.

pcs.name: Pelvic congestion — left ovarian reflux
pcs.description: Dilated, refluxing left ovarian vein with parametrial congestion.
pcs.impression: Dilated, incompetent left ovarian vein with sustained reflux and congested left parametrial plexus — findings consistent with pelvic venous reflux (pelvic congestion).
pcs.rec: Refer to interventional radiology for consideration of ovarian vein embolization.

mayThurner.name: May-Thurner pattern (left CIV)
mayThurner.description: Left common iliac vein compression with collaterals — screening positive.
mayThurner.impression: Narrowing of the left common iliac vein with elevated cross-stenosis velocity ratio and pelvic collaterals, suggestive of May-Thurner (non-thrombotic iliac vein) compression. Ultrasound is screening only — cross-sectional/IVUS confirmation advised.
mayThurner.rec: Confirm with IVUS or CT/MR venography prior to any intervention.

nutcracker.name: Nutcracker pattern (left renal vein)
nutcracker.description: Left renal vein compression — screening positive.
nutcracker.impression: Aortomesenteric compression of the left renal vein with elevated peak-velocity and diameter ratios and a beak sign, suggestive of nutcracker phenomenon. Ultrasound is screening only — CT/MR venography confirmation advised.
nutcracker.rec: Confirm with CT/MR venography and correlate with renocaval pressure gradient.
